1.explanation: HTML stands for Hyper Text Markup Language, the standard markup language for creating web pages.

1.questionText: CSS specificity of an ID selector is higher than a class selector.
1.explanation: ID selectors (100) have higher specificity than class selectors (10) in the CSS cascade.

1.questionText: Which HTML tag is used to define an unordered list?
1.explanation: <ul> defines an unordered (bulleted) list; <li> defines each list item within it.

1.questionText: The 'box-sizing: border-box' property includes padding and border in an element's total width and height.
1.explanation: border-box makes width/height calculations include content, padding, and border, avoiding layout surprises.

1.questionText: What will `console.log(typeof null)` output in JavaScript?
1.explanation: This is a well-known JavaScript quirk: typeof null returns "object" due to a legacy bug in the language.

options.A: 5, because JS coerces the string to a number
options.B: "23", because + concatenates when either operand is a string
options.C: NaN, because you can't add a string and a number

1.explanation: The + operator concatenates strings when either operand is a string, so "2" + 3 becomes "23".

1.questionText: Which CSS property is used to create space between flex items along the main axis?
1.explanation: The `gap` property (or `column-gap`) creates spacing between flex or grid items directly.

1.questionText: Semantic HTML elements like <article> and <nav> primarily exist to improve accessibility and SEO, not visual styling.
1.explanation: Semantic tags convey meaning to browsers, assistive tech, and search engines — they carry no default visual difference from a <div>.

1.questionText: In JavaScript, what does `const` guarantee about a variable?

options.A: The value can never change in any way
options.B: The binding cannot be reassigned, but object/array contents can still mutate
options.C: It is hoisted and initialized to undefined

1.explanation: const prevents reassignment of the variable binding, but if the value is an object or array, its properties/elements can still be mutated.

options.A: [2, 4, 6], because map mutates the original array
options.B: [1, 2, 3], because map returns a new array and leaves the original unchanged
options.D: An error, because map requires a second argument

1.explanation: Array.prototype.map() is non-mutating — it returns a brand-new array, leaving the original array untouched.

1.questionText: What is the purpose of the `alt` attribute on an tag?

options.B: It provides alternative text for screen readers and when the image fails to load
options.C: It defines the image's alt-format (e.g. png/jpg)
options.D: It sets a tooltip only, with no accessibility purpose

1.explanation: The alt attribute is read by screen readers and displayed if the image can't load — it's central to web accessibility.

1.questionText: CSS Grid and Flexbox solve the exact same layout problems, so there's never a reason to prefer one over the other.
1.explanation: Flexbox is one-dimensional (row or column), while Grid is two-dimensional (rows and columns together) — they suit different layout needs.
